'''James Bentley''' (born 19 October 1997) is an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr|lf}} or {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Bentley and Keyes set for Bulls début against Oldham">{{cite web|url=http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportbulls/14693811.Bentley_and_Keyes_set_for_Bulls_debut_against_Oldham|title=Bentley and Keyes set for Bulls début against Oldham|publisher=Telegraph & Argus|date=2016-08-19|accessdate=2016-09-20}}</ref>

He played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Bentley played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] in the [[Super League]] and spent time on loan from [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Bentley was born in [[Leeds]], West Yorkshire, England.{{cn|date=July 2025}}

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Bentley started his path towards a professional Rugby League career at Featherstone Rovers in the scholarship ranks after coming from nearby, Oulton Raiders. After his first season with the club, no game time and a change of coaching staff, he followed the fellow coaching staff to Bradford Bulls.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Bradford Bulls===
Bentley came up through the Bradford Bulls academy and made his initial appearance for the Bradford Bulls in 2016 against the [[Castleford Tigers]] in a pre season game and went on to make sporadic appearances throughout [[2016 RFL Championship|2016]]. Bentley cemented his place in the Bradford Bulls first team in [[2017 RFL Championship|2017]]. Despite the club's 12 point deduction, Bentley impressed scoring 18 tries. After the Bradford Bulls were relegated, Bentley signed for [[Super League]] side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===St Helens===
At the end of the 2017 season Bentley signed a two-year deal with [[Super League]] side [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. He joined the [[Sheffield Eagles]] on [[Rugby League Dual registration]] in 2018 as part of a deal between the two clubs.<ref name="Saints quartet become first dual-registrated players in 2018">{{cite web|url=http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50794/saints-quartet-become-first-dual-registered-players-in-|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180201075339/http://www.sheffieldeagles.com/article/50794/saints-quartet-become-first-dual-registered-players-in-|url-status=dead|archive-date=1 February 2018|title=Bentley joins Eagles on dual-registration|date=31 January 2018|accessdate=31 January 2018}}</ref> He played for Sheffield against the [[Dewsbury Rams]].
He played in St Helens 8-4 [[2020 Super League Grand Final]] victory over Wigan at the [[Kingston Communications Stadium]] in Hull.  He signed a one-year contract extension with St Helens for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55072997|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan 4-8 St Helens|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 4 May 2021, he was ruled out for an indefinite period after suffering a badly broken leg.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-bentley-st-helens-injury-20520186/|title=St Helens confirm brutal James Bentley injury blow as scans confirm the worst|website=www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk|access-date=19 July 2021|archive-date=4 May 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210504094941/https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-bentley-st-helens-injury-20520186|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Leeds===
On 21 June 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[Super League]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Sky Sports|date=21 Jun 2021|accessdate=5 Oct 2021|title=James Bentley: Leeds Rhinos sign St Helens back-rower on two-year deal from start of next season|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12211/12335419/james-bentley-leeds-rhinos-sign-st-helens-back-rower-on-two-year-deal-from-start-of-next-season}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Bentley made his club debut for Leeds against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] but was sent off after only 16 minutes on the field.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/60320806.amp/|title=Super League: Leeds Rhinos 20-22 Warrington Wolves - Daryl Powell's Wire edge out 12-man hosts|website=www.bbc.com|date=12 February 2022}}</ref>
On 23 May 2022, Bentley was suspended for three matches in relation to a dangerous high tackle during Leeds victory over [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61552640|title=James Bentley: Leeds Rhinos back row banned for three games after high tackle|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
In round 16 of the 2022 Super League season, Bentley was sent to the sin bin for a professional foul during Leeds 42-12 loss against his former club St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61881809?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=KARANGA/|title=Super League: St Helens 42-12 Leeds Rhinos|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
In the 2022 Semi-Final, Bentley scored two tries for Leeds in a shock 20-8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] which sent the club into the Grand Final.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/16/leeds-pull-out-stunning-win-at-wigan-to-reach-grand-final/|title=Leeds stun Wigan to reach Grand Final as remarkable resurgence goes on|newspaper=The Guardian|date=16 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Bentley played for Leeds in their 24-12 loss to [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[2022 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>
Bentley represented Ireland at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]] and played in all three group stage matches. On 14 November, the Rugby Football League placed Bentley under investigation after he was alleged to have been involved in a fight with England and [[Sydney Roosters]] player [[Victor Radley]] at England's team hotel.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.leeds-live.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-ace-james-bentley-25515007/|title=Leeds Rhinos ace James Bentley involved in alleged punch-up with England's Victor Radley|website=www.leeds-live.co.uk}}{{Dead link|date=August 2025 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63630751|title=Rugby League: Victor Radley and James Bentley investigated after alleged incident in Manchester|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
Bentley played a total of 17 games for Leeds in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos-2023-season-review-picking-out-the-best-and-worst-players-performances-results-4355920/|title=Leeds Rhinos' 2023 season review: picking out the best and worst players, performances, results|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

Bentley played 14 matches for Leeds in the 2024 Super League which saw the club finish 8th on the table. On 16 October, Bentley was released from his contract with Leeds due to personal reasons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-rhinos-release-james-bentley/|title=Leeds Rhinos release star forward as shock exit confirmed in 82-word statement|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
On 30 October 2025, Super League club [[Warrington Wolves]] announced that they had signed him on a twelve-month contract.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=30 Oct 2025|title=Warrington Wolves complete double swoop as James Bentley makes Super League move|author=Callum Walker|url=https://www.totalrl.com/warrington-wolves-complete-double-swoop-as-james-bentley-makes-super-league-move/}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-10-30 |title=Warrington complete double signing of Bentley and Smith |url=https://warringtonwolves.com/news/2025/october/Warrington-complete-double-signing-of-Bentley-and-Smith/ |access-date=2025-10-30 |website=Warrington Wolves}}</ref>
